references.title: Artificial neural network model to predict student performance using nonpersonal information

extracted.Data sources:
LMS logs, 
Assessment Scores, 
Course Information
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
authors extract the logs from the relevant tables available in OULAD. 

Authors use correlations and Feature Importances to dientiffy what the most relevant  features were.
extracted.Models:
MLP, 
SVM, 
Naive Bayes,
Random Forest
extracted.Comments: Authors use OULAD to predict performance at the course’s halfway point without using any personable identifiable information or aggregate characteristics.
extracted.Limitations:
Does not consider potentiallyrelevant information.

Holdout method

Only considers Accuracy as work’s porformance metric.

Considers all course-student pairs as independent from one another.

Sample size not specified - authors mention 32593 OULAD students, but test dataset includes a very small subset (despite the alleged 70/30 split).

annotations.text: Authors propose a Neural Network arquitecture that may be capable iof predictiong student performance without using personal sensitive information. Authors use OULAD for the purpose. Results on multiple relevant metrics are shown.
